tagline: A Spirit Filled Community in Jamshedpur
missionStatement: Stage based growth and equipping lay people to become disciple makers.
visionStatement: Connecting a million hearts globally and growing 10,000 disciples by 2030.

title: Handling Placements & Interviews — Panel Talk
description: A practical and encouraging panel talk to help students and young professionals navigate job placements and interviews with confidence and faith. Hear from those who have walked this journey and come with your questions.

title: Parenting Course — Every Saturday
description: A heart-transforming parenting course held every Saturday from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM. Discover biblical principles for raising children with love, wisdom and purpose. Running June through September — join any Saturday.
location: Zoom — every Saturday 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM IST

title: Retreat — The Joy of Serving
description: A Spirit-filled annual retreat centred on the theme: The Joy of Serving. Three days of rest, worship, teaching, and deep encounters with God as we rediscover the joy that comes from living a life poured out for others.
location: Jamshedpur — venue shared with registered members

title: YLF Walkathon
description: Join us for the YLF Walkathon — a community walk that brings together our church family and friends in a spirit of joy, health, and fellowship. All ages welcome. Let's walk together for a cause greater than ourselves.
location: Jamshedpur — route details shared upon registration

title: Retreat — Hurts, Bitterness & Forgiveness
description: A deeply healing retreat focused on one of the most transforming journeys any believer can take — releasing hurts, letting go of bitterness, and walking in the fullness of forgiveness. Come and be free.
location: Jamshedpur — venue shared with registered members

title: Christmas Programs — Caroling & Celebrations
description: Celebrate the birth of Yeshua with the whole YLF family! Join us for Christmas caroling, community celebrations, and a special Christmas service filled with worship, joy, and the warmth of family. All are welcome.
location: Jamshedpur — details shared closer to date

title: Navigating Life's Storms
excerpt: Life doesn't always go according to plan. But in every storm, there is an anchor — and His name is Yeshua. Anjalee shares her journey of finding peace in the midst of uncertainty.

children.text: Life doesn't always go according to plan. In fact, more often than not, it throws curveballs we never saw coming — a lost job, a broken relationship, a health scare, an unexpected grief. And in those moments, our first instinct is often to ask: Where is God in all of this?
"When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you." — Isaiah 43:2

I have walked through seasons that felt like standing in the middle of a storm with no shelter in sight. A season where every morning felt heavy and every evening brought more questions than answers. But it was in that very season that I discovered something I had read in Scripture all my life — God is not absent in our storms. He is present in them.
The Anchor That Holds

There is a verse in Hebrews that speaks of hope as an anchor for the soul — firm and secure (Hebrews 6:19). An anchor doesn't stop the storm. It doesn't calm the waves. But it holds the ship in place so the storm cannot carry it away. That is what hope in Yeshua does for us.
When I stopped asking God to remove the storm and started asking Him to be with me in it, everything shifted. Not the circumstances — but my posture. My peace. My ability to breathe.
What to Do in the Storm

First, acknowledge what you are feeling. God is not afraid of your tears, your anger, or your confusion. He is big enough to hold all of it. Second, return to the Word. Not as a formula, but as a lifeline. Let it remind you of who God is when everything around you is telling you otherwise. Third, lean on community. You were never meant to weather your storms alone. The body of Christ exists for such a time as this.

If you are in a storm today, I want you to know — you are not abandoned. The One who spoke peace to the waves on the Sea of Galilee is speaking peace over your situation right now. Trust Him. He has not let go of you.

author: Anjalee

title: Worship in Everyday Places: Finding God in Open Homes
excerpt: We often think of worship as something that happens in a church building on a Sunday. But what if worship was meant to happen around a kitchen table, on a Zoom call, in the middle of an ordinary week?

children.text: There is a moment in Acts 2 that I keep coming back to. After the outpouring of the Holy Spirit on the day of Pentecost, the early believers didn't just gather in the temple. They gathered in homes. They broke bread. They shared meals. They prayed. And God kept adding to their number.
"They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts, praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people." — Acts 2:46–47
This is where Open Homes began — not as a programme, but as a way of life. And I believe it is one of the most powerful expressions of worship that exists today.
Worship Is More Than Music

We often reduce worship to singing. And yes — singing is worship. But Romans 12:1 calls us to offer our whole lives as living sacrifices. That means the way we open our homes, the way we listen to a friend who is struggling, the way we make space for someone who feels alone — all of this is worship.

When we gather in an Open Home — whether physically or on Zoom — something sacred happens. People lower their guards. Conversations go deeper. The Holy Spirit moves in the ordinary. And God is glorified not just through songs, but through the sound of authentic community.
An Invitation

If you have never attended an Open Home, I want to extend a personal invitation. Come as you are. Bring your questions, your stories, your laughter, and even your doubts. You will find a family waiting to welcome you. And in that space, you may discover — as I did — that God was always closer than you thought.

author: Jaison

title: YLF Retreat March 2026 – Clarity of Sight
excerpt: The YLF Retreat March 2026 was not just an event — it was an encounter. Under the theme 'Clarity of Sight', God met us in ways that words can barely contain. Here is a glimpse of what happened.

children.text: Going into the retreat, many of us carried questions. Questions about direction, about calling, about what the next season looked like. We came carrying the weight of uncertainty. And God met us there — not with all the answers, but with something better: clarity of sight.
"I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my loving eye on you." — Psalm 32:8
What the Theme Meant

Clarity of Sight is not about having a five-year plan. It is about seeing what God sees — seeing yourself the way He sees you, seeing others the way He sees them, and seeing your circumstances through the lens of His faithfulness rather than your fear.

Over three days, through teaching, worship, prayer, and deeply honest conversations, God began to lift the fog. People who came in confused left with a renewed sense of purpose. People who came in discouraged left with fresh fire.
Moments That Marked Us

One of the most powerful moments came during an open prayer session on the second evening. Person after person began to release things they had been holding onto for years — fears, offences, false identities. And in that sacred space, the Holy Spirit brought healing that only He can bring.
We left the retreat changed. Not because of anything we did — but because of who showed up when we gathered in His name. Thank you, Lord, for clarity of sight.

author: Angelin

title: When Hope Touched His Robe
excerpt: There is a woman in the Gospels who had been sick for twelve years. Twelve years of trying everything. And then one day, she reached out and touched the hem of Yeshua's garment — and everything changed.

children.text: Twelve years is a long time to wait. Twelve years of doctors who couldn't help. Twelve years of spending everything she had. Twelve years of growing weaker, more isolated, more invisible. And yet, when she heard that Yeshua was passing by, something rose up within her. Something that had been buried under all those years of disappointment.
"She said to herself, 'If I only touch his cloak, I will be healed.'" — Matthew 9:21
The Audacity of Hope

What strikes me most about this woman is not that she was healed — though that is glorious. What strikes me is that she dared to hope again. After twelve years of disappointment, she still believed that one touch could change everything. That is not naivety. That is faith.

There are seasons in our lives where hoping feels dangerous. Where we have been let down so many times that hope itself feels like a risk we cannot afford. But this woman's story tells us something profound: it is always worth reaching out to Yeshua.
He Felt It

In the middle of a crowd pressing in on all sides, Yeshua stopped and asked, 'Who touched me?' The disciples thought the question was absurd — everyone was touching Him. But He knew. He always knows when someone reaches out in genuine faith. He never misses it.
Whatever you are reaching out to Him for today — reach. He sees you. He feels it. And He has the power to make you whole.

author: Reena

title: The Empowerment Through the Resurrection of Christ
excerpt: The resurrection is not just a historical event we celebrate at Easter. It is the source of a power that is available to every believer, every single day. Reethi explores what it means to live in resurrection power.

children.text: The moment Yeshua walked out of that tomb, something changed forever. Death was defeated. Sin lost its power. And a new kind of life became available — not just after we die, but right now, in this body, in this world, in the middle of our ordinary days.
"I want to know Christ — yes, to know the power of his resurrection and participation in his sufferings." — Philippians 3:10
What Resurrection Power Looks Like

Resurrection power is not a feeling. It is not a spiritual high that fades by Monday morning. It is a steady, sustaining force that enables us to live differently than the world around us. It gives us the ability to forgive people who don't deserve it. To love people who are difficult. To get back up after we have fallen. To keep going when everything in us wants to quit.

This same power that raised Yeshua from the dead lives in every believer through the Holy Spirit (Romans 8:11). That is not a metaphor. That is a reality that Paul wants us to lay hold of and never let go.
Living in the Power of the Resurrection

To live in resurrection power means to live as someone who knows that the story is not over. That every dead thing in your life is a candidate for resurrection. Your broken dream, your fractured relationship, your lost hope — none of it is beyond the reach of the God who raises the dead.
Let the resurrection be more than something you celebrate once a year. Let it be the lens through which you see everything. You serve a risen Yeshua — and His power is at work in you.

author: Reethi

title: Open Homes: Discipling Through Love & Service
excerpt: Discipleship doesn't always happen in a classroom or a pulpit. Sometimes it happens around a table, in the middle of a conversation, when someone feels truly seen and heard. Rakesh explores the discipleship power of Open Homes.

children.text: The word 'disciple' comes from the same root as the word 'discipline' — but it is also deeply connected to the word 'relationship.' You cannot truly disciple someone you do not know. And you cannot know someone you have never made space for.
"By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another." — John 13:35
The Open Home as a Discipleship Space

An Open Home is more than a gathering. It is an intentional space where disciples are made. When we open our homes — whether physically or on Zoom — we are saying: 'You are welcome here. You matter here. And I am willing to do life with you.'

In that environment, people grow. Not because of a curriculum or a programme — though those have their place — but because they see faith lived out up close. They see how someone handles a difficult conversation with grace. They see what it looks like to pray for a neighbour. They see love in action.
Service as a Discipleship Language

At YLF, we believe that service is one of the primary languages of discipleship. When we serve one another — when we show up for each other, pray for each other, carry each other's burdens — we are participating in the most ancient and powerful form of Christian education there is.
Open your home. Open your heart. Open your schedule. And watch what God does when you make space for His people.

author: Rakesh Cheeli

title: God's Plan > Your Plan
excerpt: We spend so much energy trying to plan our lives perfectly. But what if the most beautiful chapters of your story are the ones you never planned? Sheena reflects on surrendering control to a God who plans better than we ever could.

children.text: I am a planner by nature. I like spreadsheets, timelines, and knowing what is coming next. For a long time, I thought this was a virtue. And in many ways, it is. But there came a season when my plans fell apart — and that is when I began to discover something far better than my best plan.
"For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future." — Jeremiah 29:11
When the Plan Falls Apart

The job I was counting on fell through. The relationship I thought was heading somewhere ended. The timeline I had mapped out for my life suddenly looked nothing like reality. And for a season, I grieved. I was confused. I questioned.

But slowly — through prayer, through community, through simply sitting in God's presence — I began to see something I had missed. He had not abandoned my story. He was writing a better one. One that I could not have scripted on my own because it required detours and delays I would never have chosen.
The Invitation to Surrender

Surrender is not passive. It is one of the most active, courageous things a person can do. To say to God: 'I trust You more than I trust my own understanding' — that takes faith. And it takes practice.

If your plans have fallen apart today, I want to offer you this: God's plan is not a consolation prize for when your plan fails. It is always the better story. Surrender the pen. Let Him write. You will not regret it.
God's Plan > Your Plan. Always.

author: Sheena
